articles.title: Mexico to Host Iran Players for World Cup as War Tensions Simmer
articles.summary: Mexico will serve as the Iranian national team’s World Cup base after US authorities refused to let the players stay overnight on its soil amid ongoing military tensions.

articles.title: Latest ‘Star Wars’ Film ‘Mandalorian’ Debuts at $100 Million
articles.summary: The Mandalorian and Grogu, the first film in the Star Wars franchise in seven years, was the highest-grossing movie in the US and Canada over the four-day holiday weekend with $100 million in ticket s

articles.title: Ghana to Start Buying 30% of Large Gold Mines’ Ouput From June
articles.summary: Ghana’s central bank said it plans to increase gold purchases from the West African nation’s large-scale producers to 30% of their output from 20%, starting June 1.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Gain as Crude Oil Drops on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Global stocks rose to record highs as crude oil fell after officials signaled the US was nearing a deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows. The dollar weakened.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rally as Iran Hopes Boost Risk Appetite
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose Monday as signs that the US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices below $100 per barrel and boosted risk appetite.

articles.title: Cox Group Took 54% Bridge Loan for Deal to Buy Iberdrola Mexico
articles.summary: Spanish utility Cox ABG Group SA took a 54% bridge loan to help fund a $4.2 billion asset acquisition in Mexico and placed shares as collateral.

articles.title: Czech Premier Urges Interest Rate Cut as Inflation Risks Grow

articles.title: Top Bauxite Producer Guinea to Unveil Export Controls in June
articles.summary: Guinea, the world’s biggest bauxite producer, plans to announce reforms next month to control exports of the ore used in making aluminum as it bids to bolster prices.

articles.title: Nigeria’s First Quarter GDP Eases on Slowdown in Oil Sector
articles.summary: Nigeria’s economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as growth in both its oil and non-oil sectors eased.

articles.title: Top Africa Bourse to Tighten Algo-Trading, Market-Access Rules
articles.summary: Africa’s biggest stock-exchange operator is working to tighten oversight of how brokers and trading firms access the bourse to lessen the risk of rogue algorithms and trading errors that could disrupt
